0.name: High-Achievement but Strained
0.description: Highly engaged students under sustained pressure. Intensive studying, exam-focused behavior, dependence on stimulants, reduced lifestyle balance. Lower mental health score suggests chronic stress.

interventions.text: Mandatory Pomodoro-based study break scheduling
Sleep hygiene workshops & rest tracker enrollment
Counseling referral for stress management
Caffeine reduction program with healthy alternatives

1.name: Balanced & Healthy
1.description: Strong cognitive performance, emotional stability, healthy routines, low stimulant dependency. Productive without extreme stress.

interventions.text: Maintain current routines — periodic check-ins only
Peer mentoring opportunities for higher-risk clusters
Advanced enrichment programs to sustain engagement
Leadership and collaborative project opportunities

2.name: Disciplined but Struggling
2.description: Consistent effort and healthy routines, but not translating into strong outcomes. May reflect inefficient study strategies or cognitive overload.

interventions.text: Study strategy workshops — active recall, spaced repetition
Academic tutoring and learning style assessment
Cognitive load management training
Progress tracking with structured goal-setting

3.name: Leisure-Oriented
3.description: Low academic intensity, more passive digital activity, less exam-focused. Higher sleep may indicate reduced stress or lower workload.

interventions.text: Academic engagement program with motivational incentives
Digital usage limits & structured study scheduling
Career counseling to build intrinsic motivation
Social learning groups leveraging social strengths

4.name: Burnout Risk
4.description: Chronic overload without effective recovery. Extreme caffeine, high stress and burnout, poor mental health, declining performance despite effort.

interventions.text: IMMEDIATE: Mental health crisis intervention referral
Mandatory workload reduction — academic accommodations
Professional counseling — mandatory weekly sessions
Emergency sleep & nutrition recovery support program

5.name: Productive but Inconsistent
5.description: Elevated productivity yet exam scores slightly below average. Balancing many responsibilities simultaneously. Moderate burnout but no severe collapse.

interventions.text: Exam strategy training — translate productivity to scores
Prioritization training to reduce task fragmentation
Performance consistency & focus coaching
Time management optimization workshops
